# Schedule Management Plan

## Purpose
Define how schedules are developed, baselined, monitored, and controlled.

## Audience
Project Manager, Scheduler, PMO, Delivery Leads.

## Inputs
- WBS (`docs/templates/schedule/wbs-template-waterfall.yaml`).
- Schedule baseline (`docs/templates/schedule/schedule-baseline-waterfall.xlsx`).
- Activity list (`docs/templates/schedule/activity-list-template-cross.csv`).
- Project schedule template (`docs/templates/schedule/project-schedule-template-cross-var2.md`).

## 1. Schedule Development Approach
Describe planning horizon, decomposition rules, and integration with WBS.

## 2. Project Schedule Detail
Use the project schedule template to capture activity attributes, milestones, resource loading,
and baseline approvals. Tailor the sections to match the project complexity and governance needs.

## 3. Tools & Techniques
Identify scheduling tools and techniques (CPM, rolling wave planning, critical chain).

## 4. Roles & Responsibilities
| Role | Responsibilities | Approval Authority |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

## 5. Schedule Baseline & Change Control
Define baseline approval, change requests, and rebaselining criteria.

## 6. Progress Measurement
Describe percent complete rules, milestone tracking, and earned schedule if used.

## 7. Reporting Cadence
Define reporting frequency, audience, and escalation thresholds.

## Completion Checklist
- [ ] Schedule approach and tools documented.
- [ ] Baseline and change control rules agreed.
- [ ] Progress measurement defined.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schedule plan aligns with governance and reporting needs.
- Baseline change control is defined and approved.
